summary: First matching pass for the Target Test Ice Climbers stage module `grticeclimber`: most of the hand-written assembly was replaced with C, a new stage header was added, the C object was added to the build while the residual asm object stayed present, and two grmaterial prototypes were exposed. The PR body says it resolved most of issue #617. There were no review comments; the only recorded review was approval.
what_changed: Deleted 338 lines from `asm/melee/gr/grticeclimber.s`, including the prior assembly implementations and data for many `func_80220Fxx` and `lbl_80221xxx` symbols.

Added `src/melee/gr/grticeclimber.c` with 211 lines implementing the stage callbacks, `StageCallbacks lbl_803E8A98`, `StageData lbl_803E8AF4`, initialization logic, gobj setup, callback dispatch, and several trivial callback stubs.
Added `src/melee/gr/grticeclimber.h` with prototypes for the stage init callbacks, per-ground callbacks, `func_80220FB4`, `func_80221288`, `lbl_80221208`, and related external stage functions.
Added `$(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/gr/grticeclimber.c.o` to `TEXT_O_FILES` in `obj_files.mk` while keeping `$(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/gr/grticeclimber.s.o`, indicating a split C/asm object during this pass.
Added prototypes `func_801C8CDC(void)` and `func_801C8E08(void)` to `src/melee/gr/grmaterial.h` for functions needed by the new C translation.
Converted raw stage data from `.4byte` assembly into typed C initializers: `StageCallbacks` entries and `StageData` with file path `"/GrTIc.dat"`, stage id `47`, and callback pointers.

Recreated stage startup behavior in C: sets `stage_info.unk8C.b4 = false`, `stage_info.unk8C.b5 = true`, creates ground objects for ids 0, 1, and 2, then calls common stage setup functions `func_801C39C0`, `func_801C3BB4`, `func_801C4210`, and `func_801C42AC`.

Reimplemented `func_80220FB4` as the ground-object factory/setup helper: fetches a gobj by id, clears callbacks, installs the GX link with `GObj_SetupGXLink`, conditionally installs callback fields, invokes callback0, schedules callback2 with `func_8038FD54`, and reports failure with `OSReport`.
Kept `lbl_80221208` as MWERKS inline assembly inside the new C file under `#ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO`, preserving exact codegen for a harder-to-match effect/camera/material sequence.
Represented the unknown data table formerly at `lbl_803E8B5C` as a `u32` array with a `@todo Unknown struct` note rather than inventing a structure.
smart_moves: Used typed `StageCallbacks` and `StageData` initializers to replace raw `.4byte` data without requiring all callback names or meanings to be solved first.
Preserved exact matching for the stubborn `lbl_80221208` routine by embedding MWERKS inline assembly instead of forcing a risky nonmatching C rewrite.

Kept a mixed C/asm build arrangement by adding the C object while retaining the asm object, enabling incremental decompilation of `grticeclimber` instead of requiring the whole file to be converted at once.
Documented codegen sensitivity in `func_80220FB4` with `@todo Cannot be moved below @c cb due to an inline`, warning future editors that variable ordering affects the match.
Modeled stage info bitfield writes as `stage_info.unk8C.b4` and `stage_info.unk8C.b5`, improving readability over the original `rlwimi`/byte manipulation while still matching behavior.
Left the packed table `lbl_803E8B5C` as raw `u32` data with an explicit unknown-struct todo, which is safer than inventing semantics without evidence.
decomp_lessons: For stage target-test modules, converting the callback table and `StageData` first can unlock large chunks of matching C even when some routines remain unknown or inline-asm-only.

When an assembly routine includes calls such as `HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll`, `func_8000B1CC`, `ef_Spawn`, `func_80030E44`, `func_801C53EC`, and `func_801C8CDC`, preserving it as inline asm can be an acceptable pass-1 tactic if C codegen is not yet understood.

Ground object setup patterns are reusable: get `Ground*` from `HSD_GObjGetUserData`, clear `x8_callback` and `xC_callback`, call `GObj_SetupGXLink`, conditionally store `x1C_callback`, invoke an init callback, and optionally schedule a proc callback with `func_8038FD54`.
Build-system migration can be staged: add the new `src/...c.o` next to the old `asm/...s.o` and remove only the assembly ranges that have C replacements.

Use existing common ground helpers where possible. This PR used helpers/macros such as `Ground_JObjInline1`, `func_801C8138`, `func_801C2FE0`, and `func_801C7FF8` instead of open-coding the assembly sequences.
Unknown packed data arrays should be carried forward with address labels and a clear `@todo`, preserving matchability and future searchability until the structure is identified.
naming_conventions: The module follows the stage-file naming pattern `grticeclimber.c` and `grticeclimber.h` under `src/melee/gr/`.
Unidentified functions and labels retained address-derived names such as `func_80220F10`, `func_80220FB4`, `lbl_80220F14`, `lbl_8022109C`, and `lbl_803E8A98`.
Stage callback arrays and stage data retained their original data-label names, e.g. `lbl_803E8A98` for `StageCallbacks` and `lbl_803E8AF4` for `StageData`.
Trivial callbacks were still given typed signatures such as `bool lbl_802210C8(HSD_GObj*)` returning `false` and `void lbl_802210D0(HSD_GObj*) {}`.
The new header uses the guard `MELEE_GR_GRTICECLIMBER_H`, matching the repository's all-caps path-derived include guard style.
The stage asset path was represented directly in C as `"/GrTIc.dat"`, preserving the exact original string.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed matched assembly from `asm/melee/gr/grticeclimber.s` while leaving remaining assembly and constants in place.
Used MWERKS inline assembly for `lbl_80221208` with `#pragma push`, `asm void`, `nofralloc`, and `clang-format off/on` to preserve exact instruction output.
Used a local variable ordering constraint in `func_80220FB4`; the comment states `HSD_GObj* gobj` cannot be moved below `cb` because of an inline-related matching issue.
Translated assembly callback tables into typed C data while preserving symbol names and callback pointer ordering.
Represented bit flags as C expressions such as `(1 << 30) | (1 << 31)` and `(1 << 0)` instead of raw hex where the original data permitted it.
Used `extern f32 const lbl_804DBCB8` and `extern f32 const lbl_804DBCBC` for sdata2 float constants consumed by matched C rather than moving those constants in this pass.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the PR slice.
One recorded review by `r-burns` approved the PR with an empty body.
The PR body states `Resolves most of #617` and includes `Co-authored-by: jeffm <jeffmao99@berkeley.edu>`.
searchable_terms: Cannot be moved below cb due to an inline
follow_up_queries: Inspect later PRs for completion of `grticeclimber` after this pass, especially replacement of inline asm `lbl_80221208` and any remaining assembly in `asm/melee/gr/grticeclimber.s`.
Search for identification of the unknown `lbl_803E8B5C` table structure.
Compare `grticeclimber` against other target-test stage modules such as `grtfox`, `grtfalco`, `grtkirby`, and `grtkoopa` for shared callback-table patterns.
Check whether the header signature for `func_80220F10` was later normalized, since the diff excerpt shows the C definition taking `bool arg0` while the new header declares `int`.
Look for later naming cleanup of address-derived callbacks once target-test ground object roles were understood.
